preamble: The realization of the voiced velar stop [ɡ] as a voiced uvular stop [ɢ] is often an example of allophony and complementary distribution. This process, also known as retraction, frequently occurs in the environment of back vowels where the point of articulation shifts posteriorly.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
